musicRejectionDiagnostic.claimBoundary: not accepted capture-source correlation, not materializer input, not runtimeAudibleOutputProof, not raw audio/CDB/source-path publication
musicRejectionDiagnostic.decodeWindowResult: decodeWindowInsideRawAudioCapture=false; exact-PID CDB decode instants occur around 44-46s while raw WAV data spans about 11-12s, so analysis rows are capture-window-out-of-range and stagedReplacementPreferredInDecodeWindow=false means unavailable, not measured source preference.
musicRejectionDiagnostic.nextDocsTruth: loopback wall-clock padding plus materializer raw WAV data-span/header-consistency/padding-metadata guards are accepted helper/materializer hardening; next music proof requires a new private live bundle, not reinterpretation of the old rejected bundle.
musicRejectionDiagnostic.consultReview: Carson/Hilbert/Plato review findings accepted: forged WAV headers, missing materializer wallClockPadding enforcement, and stale active-slice validation state were fixed in the touched music-proof lane.

acceptedTruth: Selected/normal UIA names and button styles now flow through a small WinUI model/helper.
BinaryPatchesPage still owns selected-key predicates, profile matching, menu-color matching, safe-copy state, and patch semantics.
Runtime UIA smoke verifies profile choices, menu color choices, and clear optional mod rows selected-state behavior.
consultReview: Specialist consult recommended a WinUI-layer selected-choice state shape and keeping safe-copy manifest logic in BinaryPatchesPage.
Adversarial review found no blocker and specifically guarded against UIA name loss, AutomationId churn, and visible/effective patch key conflation.

acceptedTruth: Relevant BEA/Onslaught Codex skills are maintainer-local routing conveniences over tracked repo docs/tools.
Public contributors do not need user-local Codex skill files to build, test, or review the repo.
Do not commit repo-local .codex/skills or Codex auth/session/cache/log/plugin runtime state.
Full Ghidra databases/backups stay local/ignored; track deterministic exports, scripts, ledgers, summaries, hashes, and compact proof summaries instead.
Junctions are allowed only under ignored overlay roots; hardlinks to hard payloads are not allowed in the repo tree.
consultReview: Specialist read-only consult recommended normal docs over copied runtime skill bodies.
Adversarial security review blocked public Ghidra DB distribution, repo-local runtime .codex publication, and hardlink-style payload distribution.
Final docs audit found stale state/readiness validation wording; accepted and fixed.

acceptedTruth: Selected-profile status/details copy now lives in a WinUI presentation helper and text-state model.
BinaryPatchesPage keeps profile matching, selected patch keys, ProfilePresetId, safe-copy manifest logic, launch, music, and online logic.
No runtime, online, music audible-output, rebuild, release, or installed-game mutation claim changed.
consultReview: Specialist consult recommended reduced presentation state and keeping selected-key semantics out of the helper.
Adversarial review caught duplicate identity risk and a pre-existing Enhanced Preview music-swap guard weakness; duplicate identity was fixed and the guard weakness is the next bounded slice.

acceptedTruth: The slice hardens tests only: constructor default, Enhanced Preview non-touch boundary, and runtime UIA preservation of a selected create-time music swap.
No product behavior, patch/mod, online, audible-output, runtime, release, or installed-game mutation claim changed.
consultReview: Specialist consult agreed with the no-behavior-change static guard direction.
Adversarial consult requested runtime UIA preservation and default index/XAML pins; accepted and implemented.

acceptedTruth: Copied-profile launch status/modifier/readiness copy now lives in a WinUI presentation helper and reduced models.
BinaryPatchesPage keeps launch preset application, launch argument construction, launch-plan validation, safe-copy launch, manifest handling, music, and online logic.
No runtime, online, music audible-output, rebuild, release, or installed-game mutation claim changed.
consultReview: Specialist consult recommended launch/status text extraction and duplicated readiness-text cleanup.
Adversarial review blocked behavior movement and required helper-boundary guards; accepted and implemented.
Docs audit reaffirmed .codex skills and full Ghidra DBs are local/runtime or ignored overlays, not tracked public source.

acceptedTruth: Seven Windowed & Mods launch preset buttons now show selected state and selected UIA names.
Selected launch-preset state is presentation-only; launch args, copied-profile launch planning, safe-copy signatures, music selection, online readiness, and AppCore calls remain separate.
Manual edits to launch-owned controls clear the selected marker; savegame and create-time music-swap choices remain independent.
Broad docs/public/hygiene gates passed and no BEA/cdb/WinUI process remained after closeout.
consultReview: Specialist consult recommended the existing selected-choice helper plus page-local presentation state and targeted manual-edit clearing.
Adversarial review blocked using selected/UIA/style state as a behavior source and requested static guards around launch args, signatures, music independence, and online wording.
Final implementation review found no blocker and noted only that the test click helper should stay limited to idempotent preset buttons.

acceptedTruth: PatchBench selected/normal style lookup now lives in PatchBenchChoiceVisualState.ApplyPatchBenchChoiceStyles.
BinaryPatchesPage still owns selected-key predicates, profile matching, menu-color matching, launch-preset selected marker, and manual-edit routing.
Runtime UIA smoke now covers launch-owned checkbox, combo, and text manual-edit clearing while preserving create-time music-swap independence.
consultReview: Specialist review recommended only a small WinUI presentation extraction and keeping predicates/manual-clear routing page-local.
Adversarial review blocked any extraction where selected-preset state could drive launch args, safe-copy prep, AppCore calls, Host/Join wording, or proof/status claims.

acceptedTruth: Asset Library browse action now says Browse export folder.
First-run guidance says to use the game install as source for the external extractor, then load the separate generated export folder.
Missing-catalog status now calls out a selected full BEA install as the game install, not the generated export folder.
Missing-catalog status now lives in AppCore AssetCatalogLoadStatusText and has an executable temp-directory regression that verifies no full local path is leaked.
Catalog resolution and AssetCatalogService loading behavior remain unchanged.
consultReview: Specialist review recommended the narrow text-only catalog-picker QOL change.
Adversarial review blocked in-app extraction/generation, game-install writes, asset bundling, private-path publication, and full 3D/rebuild/runtime overclaims.
Closeout review requested UIA browse-name alignment and executable helper coverage for the selected-game-install status branch; both were accepted and implemented.
